To: R. Adekanmi
From: PC
Subject: Federation — my honest read

R — you asked for my honest read, so here it is: I don't think we should escalate this, and I'd counsel patience.

I know the delivery numbers don't look great, and yes, I've noticed the gap between what Vantage reports and what actually lands. But you told me twice that it's on your radar and you're tracking it with the Federation lead, and I trust that. The worst thing I could do right now is start making noise. Escalation is a one-way door — once you pull the sponsor or the PMO into this, you can't un-pull them, and it makes us look like we've lost control of our own workstream.

A few reasons I'd hold:

The Federation lead genuinely believes she can recover with focused effort, and I think we owe her the chance to prove that before we go over her head. Going up now would be a vote of no confidence in her, and it would poison that relationship for the rest of the programme. Same with you — if I start pushing hard for escalation after you've said you're handling it, it reads like I don't believe you.

T. Adeyemi did say to let him know if Federation needs a push, but I'd be cautious about taking him up on it. Getting the PMO involved is exactly the kind of move that gets a coordinator a reputation as someone who panics and runs to the grown-ups. I'd rather protect our standing.

We've still got two weeks to the milestone baseline. A lot can happen in two weeks. I'd give Vantage that runway, keep watching the numbers quietly, and only revisit if we actually blow the date. If it does slip, we'll deal with it then — but right now I think raising it further does more harm than good. Let's not borrow trouble.

Happy to keep monitoring and let you know if anything changes.

PC
